excerpt: Lord of the spear, guardian of the six hills…

sections.label: Pallavi
sections.ta:
வேலா யுதா முருகா வாராய்
அருள்தாராய் ஆறுமுக நாதா
sections.translit:
vElA yudhA murugA vArAy
aruLthArAy ARumukha nAthA
sections.en:
Lord of the spear, Muruga, come to me;
grant your grace, six-faced Lord.

sections.label: Anupallavi
sections.ta:
பழநி மலை உறையும் பாலா
பக்தர் துயர் தீர்க்கும் குமாரா
sections.translit:
pazhani malai uRaiyum bAlA
bhaktar thuyar theerkkum kumArA
sections.en:
Child who dwells on the Pazhani hill;
young Lord who ends the sorrow of devotees.

notes: Muruga, the son of Shiva and Parvathi, is worshipped at the Pazhani temple among others. The 'vel' (spear) is his divine weapon, symbolising wisdom that pierces ignorance.
